code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test05-APR-2018Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and mild ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy, despite a low HbA1c. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Further nephrological evaluation and assessment for non-diabetic causes of renal dysfunction are recommended.
